title: Validate Binary Search Tree
prompt:
Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid binary search tree (BST).

A BST is valid if:
- All nodes in the left subtree of a node are strictly less than the node's value.
- All nodes in the right subtree are strictly greater than the node's value.
- Both subtrees are themselves valid BSTs.

If the tree is empty, return true. Duplicate values are not allowed.
constraintQuestions: Are duplicates allowed in the BST? (No.)
What should we return for an empty tree? (true.)
Do all left subtree values need to be less than the node, not just the immediate child? (Yes.)
Do all right subtree values need to be greater than the node, not just the immediate child? (Yes.)

walkthrough.title: DFS with min/max bounds
walkthrough.body:
Use depth-first search and carry a valid range for each node.
- The root starts with (-Infinity, Infinity).
- The left child must be in (min, node.val).
- The right child must be in (node.val, max).
If any node violates its bounds, return false.

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(n) because each node is visited once.
Space: O(h) for recursion stack, where h is the tree height.
